message:
feat(error): respect --no-color / NO_COLOR / TTY detection when rendering human errors

Previously, `handle_error` always derived its color decision at call time
from `io::stderr().is_terminal()` alone, ignoring both the `--no-color`
CLI flag and the `NO_COLOR` environment convention. This caused ANSI
escapes to leak into piped output for users who had deliberately opted
out of color, and it meant the decision was recomputed at every error
site rather than matching the one used for normal output.

- Add a pure helper `should_color_human_errors(no_color_flag,
  no_color_env_present, stderr_is_terminal)` so the precedence rules are
  unit-testable without mocking the process environment.
- Compute the color decision once in `main()` from the parsed `Cli` plus
  `NO_COLOR` / stderr TTY state, and thread it through every
  `handle_error` call (bootstrap, advisory lock, auto-import, terminal
  command result, etc.).
- Update `handle_error` to accept the precomputed `color_mode` so the
  human branch renders `structured.to_human(color_mode)` consistently.
- Add regression coverage:
    * `--no-color` flag suppresses color even on a TTY
    * `NO_COLOR` env var suppresses color even on a TTY
    * Non-TTY stderr suppresses color regardless of flag/env
    * Only TTY + no flag + no env enables color

Matches the "no color on pipes, honor NO_COLOR" expectation already
documented for normal CLI output and keeps error rendering aligned with
it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
